questions.text: What is the difference between Autopilot and Supervised mode in Kiro?

options.label: Autopilot executes actions autonomously, while Supervised mode lets the user review and approve actions before execution
Vibe mode is faster because it skips validations, while Autopilot validates each step
Autopilot only works with specs, while Vibe mode works with any prompt
There is no functional difference, only the name changes depending on the Kiro version

questions.explanation: Kiro offers two interaction modes: Autopilot allows the agent to act autonomously without confirmation at each step, while Supervised mode requires user approval before executing actions. Vibe sessions are a different type of session (interactive Q&A), not an execution mode.

questions.text: Checkpoints automatically save the complete state of the codebase at every interaction.

options.label: True
False

questions.explanation: Checkpoints save only the changes made by Kiro during the current session, not the complete state of the codebase. This allows reverting specific changes made by the agent without affecting the rest of the project.

questions.text: What happens when you restore a checkpoint in Kiro?

options.label: Reverts all file changes made by the agent after that point, preserving the conversation history
Erases all conversation history and restores files to the project's initial state
Creates a new git branch with the file state at that checkpoint
Only marks the point in the conversation without reverting any files

questions.explanation: When restoring a checkpoint, all file changes made by the agent after that point are reverted. However, conversation history up to that checkpoint is preserved, allowing you to see what was discussed and decided.

questions.text: You want to experiment with a risky refactoring without losing current progress. Which Kiro feature is most appropriate?

options.label: Create a new git branch before starting
Use Checkpoints to mark the current state and revert if necessary
Manually copy files to a local backup
Use the /undo command to undo each change individually

questions.explanation: Checkpoints allow experimenting freely knowing you can revert with one click if the result is unsatisfactory. File state is saved automatically, eliminating the need for manual backups or auxiliary branches.

questions.text: What is the role of Subagents in Kiro's flow?

options.label: Third-party plugins that add extra functionality to the editor
Specialized agents that handle specific tasks maintaining their own local context, preventing context rot
Smaller versions of the main agent that run in parallel to speed up responses
Debugging tools that monitor the main agent's behavior

questions.explanation: Subagents are specialized agents that handle specific tasks maintaining their own local context. This prevents context rot (context degradation) that occurs when a single agent accumulates too much information from different domains.

questions.text: Custom subagents allow separating context between frontend and backend, avoiding context window pollution.

options.label: True
False

questions.explanation: Custom subagents allow creating focused agents (e.g., frontend-agent and backend-agent), each with its own isolated context. This prevents information from one domain polluting the other's context, keeping responses more precise and relevant.

questions.text: The agent made changes to 5 files but the result was not as expected. You want to return to the previous state without losing conversation history. How to proceed?

options.label: Use git reset --hard to revert all files
Ask the agent to manually undo each change
Restore the previous checkpoint, which reverts files but keeps the conversation
Close the session and open a new conversation from scratch

questions.explanation: Restoring the previous checkpoint reverts all file changes made by the agent after that point but preserves conversation history. This allows you to keep the context of what was discussed while returning to the previous file state.

questions.text: What is the main advantage of Subagents over the main agent when executing complex tasks across multiple domains?

options.label: Subagents maintain isolated context per domain, preventing context rot and enabling parallel task execution
Subagents are faster because they use smaller and cheaper models
Subagents can access tools that the main agent cannot
Subagents automatically save checkpoints between each operation

questions.explanation: Subagents allow Kiro to run multiple tasks in parallel or delegate specific tasks to specialized agents. The main advantage is isolated context: each subagent maintains its own context, preventing context rot that occurs when a single agent accumulates too much information from different domains.
